hook.text: An addiction recovery app tests gamification. Streaks and rewards boost engagement — but also compulsive behavior.

buildup.text: For general audiences, streaks motivate. For people with addictive tendencies, they can trigger harmful patterns.

discovery.text: Health apps, financial tools, and kids' products serve vulnerable users. Standard A/B rules don't cover the risks.

twist.text: Engagement metrics can go up while user wellbeing goes down. More screen time isn't always a win.

climax.text: Measure wellbeing, not just engagement. For sensitive products, add harm metrics alongside conversion metrics.

punchline.text: Engagement isn't always good. Measure wellbeing for vulnerable users.

quiz.question: Why is A/B testing riskier with vulnerable populations?
options: Engagement-boosting features can trigger harmful behaviors in sensitive users
Vulnerable populations always have smaller sample sizes
Testing tools don't work with accessibility features
